title: BlackBerry Stock Investor Sentiment and Market Analysis
text: BlackBerry holds a sentiment score of 63 out of 100, though this is down compared to the 30-day moving average. BlackBerry overperforms a majority of its industry peers when it comes to sentiment, ranking in the 70th percentile. The Canadian technology company has delivered quarterly financial results suggesting stability through its QNX division, which makes software embedded in 275 million vehicles worldwide. Total revenues have been improving for three consecutive quarters, with 10% growth in the fiscal fourth quarter, and BlackBerry has reported positive net earnings for four consecutive quarters. However, there are mixed signals from the market. The Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio is 70.85, suggesting a rich valuation that may limit upside. The current share price is brushing against the most bullish forecasts from analysts. According to 4 analysts, the average rating for BB stock is Hold, with a 12-month stock price target of $4.85, representing a 21.52% decrease from recent prices. The percentage of shares sold short for BlackBerry has grown since its last report, though this doesn't necessarily mean the stock will fall in the near term.
